segments.text: [Speaker Notes] Chapter 22, Opener, DNA replication is catalyzed by a dynamic multiprotein machine called the replisome. DNA polymerase, which catalyzes phosphodiester bond formation, is but one of the many proteins that must act in concert to faithfully copy each of the two DNA strands in a single replication fork.
